category: Related-product variant data precedes main coffee variant data

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty, non-'unknown' labels are supported by the page text. Product title, roaster, location, origin, region, producer, process, variety, altitude, harvest, roast, tasting notes, producer text, and default variant price/size/package grams are explicitly stated. Availability is supported by the presence of quantity and 'Add to cart'. 'is_blend': false is supported because a single origin/country/region/producer/variety coffee is described with no indication of blending. 'is_espresso': false is supported because the product is presented as filter-forward and not labeled espresso; the separate 'You may also like' espresso product is distinct. 'is_decaf': false and 'is_coferment_or_infused': false are supported by absence of any decaf/coferment/infused indication alongside a standard washed Bourbon offering. 'is_specialty_coffee': true is supported by the specialty roaster context and detailed single-lot coffee attributes on the product page.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as an html-to-text render of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is exactly what you'd expect: header/nav, cross-sell product, breadcrumb, product title, price, size selector, quantity/add-to-cart, origin/spec details, descriptive copy, brew recommendation, shipping/grind note, variant data, and footer. The coffee details are coherent and specific without feeling overengineered: Rwanda / Nyamasheke / Kanzu washing station / Bourbon / washed / 1,800–2,100 masl / seasonal harvest all fit together well for a specialty roaster. Tasting notes and copy are believable and in the right register for coffee marketing. The variant block is also internally consistent: sizes, SKUs, names, and gram weights align, and the pricing looks realistic for 250g and 1kg specialty coffee. Even the cross-sell block has plausible variant metadata. Minor tiny tell: 'Bourbon' for Rwanda is common enough in retail shorthand, though some roasters might use more specific cultivar language; otherwise this is indistinguishable from a real page.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: Kanzu Washing Station + surrounding smallholder farmers
label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting Notes: red currant, orange marmalade, black tea, cane sugar

In the cup we find the classic sparkle we love from western Rwanda: red currant up front, orange marmalade sweetness through the middle, and a clean black-tea finish.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: red currant, orange marmalade, black tea, cane sugar
label.coffee.producer_text: This lot comes from the Kanzu washing station on the steep green hills above Lake Kivu. Farmers deliver hand-picked cherry in the afternoon, and the station team sorts, depulps, ferments, washes, and slow-dries the parchment on raised beds.
